rulings.Case Title: MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. COURTHOUSE VENTURES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Approving Interim Report Of Receiver And Distribution Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2020 line 3. OTHER MICHAEL PECHERER ESQ Petition For Order Approving Interim Report Of Receiver And Distribution Order Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Receiver's Petition for Distribution Order. Hearing Required. Parties to meet and confer prior to the hearing to finalize their discussions regarding the appointment of a Special Master. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. LASHEA RUSSELL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2020 line 8. Motion For Entry Of Judgment Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Entry of Judgment. Peremptory Challenge filed by the defendant is denied as untimely. The motions pending before the judge include a continued Motion for Entry of Judgment and an advanced motion to vacate the settlement agreement, both motions have been previously considered by the judge resulting in continuance and advancement as the result of such consideration for the related motions to be heard at the same time. Plaintiff's Motion for Entry of Judgment. Hearing Required. =(501/CFH)
